steps.title: Find the inserted phrase
steps.content: The phrase "among their many roles" interrupts the sentence between that and provide. Because it is extra information, it needs matching punctuation before and after the interruption.

steps.title: Reject mismatched marks
steps.content: Choice A uses a dash before a phrase closed with a comma. Choice B uses a semicolon, which cannot separate that from its verb. Choice D uses a colon, which does not set off the interruption.

steps.title: Pair the commas
steps.content: A comma after that pairs with the comma after roles, neatly setting off "among their many roles." The answer is C.
